Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Xkcd thing, now interactive 1181 upvotes, 153 comments
  2. The largest acidic geyser has been putting on quite a show 22 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Physics Girl: Super-Kamiokande – Imaging the sun by detecting neutrinos [video] 439 upvotes, 69 comments
  4. We've freed Cookie's Bustle from copyright hell 100 upvotes, 12 comments
  5. TorchLean: Formalizing Neural Networks in Lean 78 upvotes, 10 comments
  6. Motorola GrapheneOS devices will be bootloader unlockable/relockable 125 upvotes, 25 comments
  7. An Interactive Intro to CRDTs (2023) 102 upvotes, 20 comments
  8. Textadept 85 upvotes, 16 comments
  9. Voxile: A ray-traced game made in its own engine and programming language 127 upvotes, 29 comments
  10. Time, Space, and Life as We Know It (2017) 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. Graphics Programming Resources 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Launch HN: Cekura (YC F24) – Testing and monitoring for voice and chat AI agents 72 upvotes, 19 comments
  13. CIA working to arm Kurdish forces to spark uprising in Iran, sources say 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Claude's Cycles [pdf] 512 upvotes, 222 comments
  15. Lenovo’s new ThinkPads score 10/10 for repairability 278 upvotes, 118 comments
  16. Disable Your SSH access accidentally with scp 105 upvotes, 52 comments
  17. You can use newline characters in URLs 32 upvotes, 13 comments
  18. 130k Lines of Formal Topology: Simple and Cheap Autoformalization for Everyone? 16 upvotes, 5 comments
  19. My take on vibe coding for PMs 21 upvotes, 8 comments
  20. Don't become an engineering manager 319 upvotes, 231 comments
  21. GPT‑5.3 Instant 306 upvotes, 235 comments
  22. Intel's make-or-break 18A process node debuts for data center with 288-core Xeon 255 upvotes, 209 comments
  23. Mac external displays for designers and developers, part 2 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  24. Weave – A language aware merge algorithm based on entities 18 upvotes, 9 comments
  25. MacBook Pro with M5 Pro and M5 Max 696 upvotes, 688 comments
  26. When AI writes the software, who verifies it? 166 upvotes, 151 comments
  27. What’s in a name? (2014) 11 upvotes, 5 comments
  28. Show HN: AgentBus – Centralized AI Agent-to-Agent Messaging via REST API 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Mount Mayhem at Netflix: Scaling Containers on Modern CPUs 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. TikTok won't protect DMs with E2EE, saying it would put users at risk 4 upvotes, 1 comments

Last generated 11:30am, 4 March 2026. Source here.
